sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.114 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: A finite group$G$is called { weakly ab-maximal} if$|H:[H,H]| |G:[G,G]|$for all$H G$. Do weakly ab-maximal groups have bounded derived length? It is known that weakly ab-maximal groups are direct products of weakly ab-maximal$p$-groups (F.\,Lisi, L.\,Sabatini, { J.~Group Theory}, 27 (2024), 1203--1217).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
